channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: American released from Syrian prison is flown out of the country, a US official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has transported out of Syria an American who disappeared seven months ago into former President Bashar Assad’s notorious prison system and was among the thousands released this week by rebels, a U.S. official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Thousands of Syrians celebrate in central Damascus during first Friday prayers since Assad's fall
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Thousands of Syrians gathered Friday in Damascus' historic main mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since the ouster of President Bashar Assad, while giant crowds celebrated in the capital's largest square and across the country.
item.dc:creator: ALBERT AJI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Conan O'Brien's parents die within days of each other
item.description: Dr. Thomas O’Brien and Ruth R. O’Brien, the parents of late-night icon Conan O’Brien, both passed away within days of each other at their home in Brookline, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ main square and a historic mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown, a major symbolic moment for the country’s dramatic change of power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ice Cube's mind stays on music with other endeavors from BIG3 to NFL partnership keeping him busy
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Ice Cube could have chased trends to keep up with today’s rap game for his latest release, but instead, he stayed loyal to the gritty West Coast gangsta sound that’s been his signature since his early N.W.A. days and flourishing solo career.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Woman who falsely accused Duke lacrosse players of rape in 2006 publicly admits she lied
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The woman who in 2006 falsely accused three Duke University lacrosse players of raping her – making national headlines that stirred tensions about race, class and the privilege of college athletes -- has admitted publicly for the first time that she made up the story.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Duke lacrosse players' rape accuser admits she lied
item.description: A woman who accused three former Duke University men’s lacrosse players of rape nearly two decades ago admitted she lied about the allegations and asked them for forgiveness.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Prosecutors will seek the death penalty for 2 Venezuelan men accused of killing Texas girl
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Prosecutors in Texas announced Friday that they will seek the death penalty against two Venezuelan men who are accused of killing a 12-year-old Houston girl after they had entered the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ocean heat killed half of population of Alaskan bird species: Report
item.description: Scientists have noticed an increase in marine heatwaves, which are periods of warmer-than-usual ocean waters.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes roughly 1,500 sentences and pardons 39 people in biggest single-day act of clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of roughly 1,500 people who were released from prison and placed on home confinement during the coronavirus pandemic and is pardoning 39 Americans convicted of nonviolent crimes.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Flight from LaGuardia diverts to JFK after bird strike: American Airlines
item.description: A flight from LaGuardia Airport to North Carolina was diverted to John F. Kennedy International Airport on Thursday evening due to a reported bird strike, according to American Airlines.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bird strike disables jetliner engine, forces emergency landing at JFK airport
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A bird strike involving an American Airlines jetliner disabled one of the plane's two engines shortly after takeoff from New York's LaGuardia Airport, forcing the flight to turn around and land at John F. Kennedy International Airport, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online 'constantly': Pew report
item.description: Nearly half of American teenagers say they are online “constantly” despite concerns about the effects of social media and smartphones on their mental health, according to a new report published Thursday by the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Dick Vitale says he's cancer-free after 4 bouts with disease in just over 3 years
item.description: Vitale had surgery in the summer to remove cancerous lymph nodes from his neck. He was previously treated for melanoma and lymphoma, and had radiation treatments last year for vocal cord cancer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Missouri man Travis Timmerman, missing since June, freed from Syrian prison
item.description: A Missouri man found in Syria said early Friday he was freed following the overthrow of Syrian President Bashar Assad after seven months in prison.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Hammer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Drones spotted over Connecticut sky in latest phenomenon
item.description: A social media user claimed to have spotted several drones in the Connecticut sky Thursday night. Sightings have become more common in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York City mayor meets with Trump's 'border czar' to discuss how to go after 'violent' criminals
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams met with President-elect Donald Trump's incoming “border czar” on Thursday, with the Democratic mayor expressing an enthusiasm to work with the incoming administration to pursue violent criminals in the city while Trump promises mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Back trouble and brain fog bothered suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing, his posts show
item.description: After Luigi Mangione made the difficult decision to undergo spinal surgery last year for chronic back pain, he became a proponent of the procedure that changed his life for the better.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department, Louisville reach deal after probe prompted by Breonna Taylor killing
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — The U.S. Justice Department and the city of Louisville have reached an agreement to reform the city’s police force after an investigation prompted by the fatal police shooting of Breonna Taylor and police treatment of protesters, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Drone operators worry that anxiety over mystery sightings will lead to new restrictions
item.description: Drones for commercial and recreational use have grown rapidly in popularity, despite restrictions on who can operate them and where they can be flown.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Dakota regulators OK underground storage for proposed Midwest carbon dioxide pipeline
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — North Dakota regulators approved permits Thursday for underground storage of carbon dioxide delivered through a massive pipeline proposed for the Midwest, marking another victory for a project that has drawn fierce opposition from landowners.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bone-chilling cold returns to the Midwest in the wake of a storm that soaked the East Coast
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Frigid weather returned to the Upper Midwest on Thursday after a storm that swept up the East Coast delivered a blow to New England, packing powerful gusts that knocked out power along with a deluge of rain and warming temperatures that washed away snow and dampened ski resort
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ: No undercover FBI agents were present for Jan. 6, countering conspiracy theories
item.description: The conclusion rejects theories that undercover FBI agents provoked the attack on the Capitol, which has since prompted charges for more than 1,500 figures involved in the riot.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's employee who called 911 in CEO's shooting is eligible for a reward, but it will take time
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — More than 400 tips were called into the New York Police Department's Crime Stoppers tip line during the five-day search for a masked gunman who ambushed and fatally shot UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson last week.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: “I think if President Biden wanted to talk to me about that, I will talk to him about it, but I don’t think I should be giving public advice on the pardon power,” Bill Clinton said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Brown snow falls over Maine town; officials warn against touching, eating it
item.description: Residents of a Maine town are being asked to avoid touching or ingesting the “brown” show that fell in the area.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KRON4

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: What Obama told Bibi
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch: Dolphins rescued after being stranded in shallow waters
item.description: The lagoon's depth was only 2 to 3 feet deep at high tide, making it difficult for the dolphins to escape the area.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year, will ring the New York Stock Exchange bell
item.description: About six months ago, Donald Trump was sitting in a courtroom in lower Manhattan listening to a jury make him the first former president convicted of a crime.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and DAVID BAUDER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill Belichick, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill finalize coaching deal
item.description: Bill Belichick has reportedly been tapped as the next head coach for the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, KRON4

item.title: Atmospheric river brings weather whiplash to East Coast as bomb cyclone develops
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The U.S. East Coast began a whiplash-inducing stretch of weather Wednesday with a deluge of rain, rapid snowmelt and powerful gusts, creating dangerous conditions, due in part to an atmospheric river and developing bomb cyclone.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Arizona AG sues Saudi firm over 'excessive' groundwater pumping, saying it's a public nuisance
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes announced Wednesday she's suing a Saudi Arabian agribusiness for allegedly violating a public nuisance law, contending that its groundwater pumping threatens the public health, safety and infrastructure of local communities in a rural western county
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luxury real estate brokers charged in federal indictment with sex trafficking in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two luxury real estate brokers and their brother have been charged with luring, drugging and violently raping dozens of women over more than a decade, according to a federal indictment unsealed Wednesday in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DA suggests unusual idea for halting Trump’s hush money case while upholding his conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Eager to preserve President-elect Donald Trump ’s hush money conviction even as he returns to office, prosecutors are suggesting various ways forward — including one based on how some courts handle criminal cases when defendants die. Trump’s spokesperson called the ideas “pathetic.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
